title: Two Sydney men jailed for Grindr robbery spree targeting gay men

Tanswell received 6 years 8 months, Leilua 6 years 2 months. Victims included men in their 50s-60s. One victim was threatened with a kitchen knife.

title: Victorian man Christian Keryakus sentenced for TikTok-inspired Grindr attacks
description: Christian Keryakus, 19, pleaded guilty to assaulting two men lured via Grindr. He used a photo of a 15-year-old to entrap victims, accused them of pedophilia, and attacked them with accomplices using a cricket bat. Inspired by TikTok vigilante videos. Sentenced to 2.5-year community corrections order with 250 hours unpaid work.

title: Five Perth teenagers sentenced for homophobic Grindr luring attacks
description: Five teenage boys sentenced in Perth Childrens Court for luring gay and bisexual men via fake Grindr profiles to parks on 18 September 2024, then ambushing them with knives, machetes, metal bars, pepper spray and tasers. Sentences ranged from 18 months to 2 years 2 months detention. Judge described attacks as planned and premeditated.

title: Homophobic and antisemitic graffiti on The Laird and Eagle Leather in Melbourne
description: Iconic LGBTIQ+ venues The Laird and Eagle Leather in Abbotsford were targeted with coordinated homophobic and antisemitic graffiti including God Hates Fags, FAG on doors, neo-Nazi Sonnenrad symbols, and Ned Kelly imagery. Same perpetrators also vandalised the Holocaust Museum in Elsternwick. Victoria Police released images of two suspects.

title: Nazi salute at Connections Nightclub in Perth - woman fined
description: Theresa Plunkett-Hill, 43, performed a Nazi salute three times and shouted Heil Hitler at a German security guard after being ejected from Connections Nightclub, an iconic LGBTIQ+ venue in Perth. She was fined $1000 and lost her job.

title: Queensland man jailed for Grindr vigilante attack in Cairns
description: Blake Nightingall, 20, pleaded guilty to luring a man via fake Grindr profile using photos of himself as a 15-year-old to Smithfield library. Victim was confronted by three people with a baseball bat, beaten, choked, and robbed. Nightingall sentenced to 10 months imprisonment with immediate parole. A GoFundMe fundraiser raised $660 for the attacker before being removed.

title: Two more Perth teenagers sentenced for Grindr luring attacks
description: Two additional Perth teenagers from the same gang sentenced for luring gay and bisexual men via Grindr. The 18-year-old received 14 months detention; the 16-year-old received 6 months for aggravated armed robbery and assault occasioning bodily harm.

title: NSW man convicted in landmark transgender hate speech case
description: Thomas Fordham, 27, convicted in first-ever NSW prosecution for gender identity vilification under Section 93Z of the Crimes Act 1900. He pleaded guilty to threatening or inciting violence on grounds of gender identity after making YouTube comments calling for genocide of transgender people. Sentenced to 12-month community correction order.

title: Melbourne man targeted with homophobic abuse and online hate in Kensington
description: Brad Cresswell-Lee, a JOY 94.9 radio newsreader, was verbally abused with homophobic slurs at JJ Holland Park in Kensington. After confronting the man and filming, the perpetrator falsely accused him of inappropriate behaviour. Cresswell-Lee then received a wave of homophobic abuse online after sharing the incident.

title: Same-sex couple brutally bashed in homophobic attack in Rundle Mall Adelaide
description: Two men in their 20s were holding hands when attacked by two teenagers in Rundle Mall at 8:30pm. One attacker pulled a balaclava over his face before launching the assault while hurling homophobic abuse. One victim was hospitalised with his jaw broken twice; the other sustained significant bruising. Both suspects were arrested and bailed to Adelaide Youth Court.

title: NRL star Reece Walsh subjected to homophobic abuse over nail polish in England
location: Liverpool, England (incident during NRL tour; Australian athlete)
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Brisbane Broncos fullback Reece Walsh was subjected to homophobic slurs and abuse from English spectators during the 2025 Kangaroos tour match in Liverpool after being sin-binned. Fans mocked him for wearing nail polish, which he credits to his four-year-old daughter.

title: Burnie councillor suspended for homophobic and racist social media posts
description: Burnie City Councillor Trent Aitken suspended for two weeks after a Code of Conduct Panel found seven homophobic statements in his social media posts made between January-May 2025, including posts criticising transgender people and opposing rainbow flags at council. He was the sole vote against an LGBTQIA+ action plan.

title: Swastikas and homophobic graffiti on Imperial Hotel and across Inner West Sydney
location: Newtown, Enmore, Erskineville, Sydney, New South Wales
description: Swastikas, homophobic slurs, and offensive graffiti were spray-painted across Newtown, Enmore, and Erskineville between 1:30am-2am, targeting 27 political posters, public structures, and the Imperial Hotel in Erskineville where a swastika was painted over signage reading proudly LGBTQIA+. Felix Kiera, 21, was later charged with 53 offences including 21 counts of displaying a Nazi symbol. He was refused bail.

title: Anti-trans activist Kirralie Smith ordered to pay $95k for vilifying trans women
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Kirralie Smith and Binary Australia were found to have unlawfully vilified two trans women football players through social media posts from 2022-2024, including publishing their photos, names, and locations. First person found to have vilified someone for being trans under NSW law. Ordered to pay $95,000 and issue public apology. Smith is appealing.

title: Wave of homophobic hate and threats following Perth 2030 Gay Games announcement
description: Announcement of Perth hosting the 2030 Gay Games was met with a wave of hateful and abusive comments online. Media outlets including OUTinPerth were forced to lock off social media comments and report threats of violence to authorities. Pride WA President Michael Felix confirmed a strong uptick in hateful comments, noting risks to transgender people, First Nations people, and people of colour within the community.

title: Transgender person assaulted by group of youths on Gold Coast tram
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] A 25-year-old transgender person was physically assaulted by a group of youths while travelling on a tram to Broadbeach on Saturday November 1. The group targeted the victim and pulled a sticker from their backpack. Brisbane Pride president James McCarthy expressed outrage. The Gold Coast Pride Collective called it a result of misinformation and vilification campaigns against the trans community.

title: Ken Doig murdered at gay beat in Toowoomba on Christmas Eve
description: Ken Doig was on his way to meet his parents for Midnight Mass on Christmas Eve 2001 when he stopped at a public toilet near Toowoomba, a known gay beat. He was bashed, hit in the head with an axe, and had his throat cut. The murder remains unsolved.

title: Robert Woodland found dead near Adelaide gay beat in suspected hate crime
description: Robert Woodland's body was discovered on December 8, 2004. His car was parked in the Veale Gardens car park, while the 36-year-old's corpse was found 700 metres away. He was last seen at the beat at 1am on December 5, after having left a nightclub. Police declared robbery as the motive despite his missing wallet and vicious injuries to his head at a place where gay men were routinely bashed. The murder remains unsolved.

title: Craig Gee brutally bashed in homophobic attack while holding boyfriend hand in Surry Hills
location: Surry Hills, Sydney, New South Wales
description: Craig Gee was attacked by four men while holding his boyfriend Shane Brennen's hand walking down Crown Street in Surry Hills. Part of his skull was reduced to powder and his leg was broken. His head was stomped several times against the pavement. One of the assailants later used Craig's stolen phone to call his mother saying 'We killed your son.' Despite CCTV evidence, police closed the case without arrests. The incident prompted community vigils and changes at Surry Hills Police.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes

title: Two men attacked by homophobic Donald Trump supporter near Newtown Sydney
description: Sam and Andrew were attacked near Newtown while in line at a McDonald's by an intoxicated man who was rambling about Donald Trump winning the election, saying things like 'how white men rule now that Trump has won.' The man physically assaulted them in what they described as a homophobic attack.

title: Three LGBTI people assaulted in transphobic attack in Footscray Melbourne
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Starlady, Canon (a trans man), and Azja were leaving a restaurant in Footscray when a man yelled 'get the fuck out' before chasing them with two friends. One grabbed Azja in a headlock, and when Canon tried to call police, he was punched in the face. Police refused to label the incident a hate crime despite the victims saying it was clearly motivated by prejudice against their gender identity.

title: Sydney drag queens rescue man from homophobic attack on Oxford Street
location: Oxford Street, Surry Hills, Sydney, New South Wales
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Ivan Flinn of Surry Hills was punched after leaving an Oxford Street bar to get a kebab, when he was harassed by a group of men. Drag queens intervened to rescue him. NSW Police confirmed officers broke up an attack involving up to seven people and were investigating a possible homophobic bias motivation.
perpetrator_info: Group of up to seven men

title: Gay men attacked at Footscray Park beat in Melbourne
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Multiple gay men were subjected to homophobic abuse, threats, and physical violence at Footscray Park in Melbourne, a known cruising area. One man was lured into a secluded area before being assaulted by a large group of men. A candlelight vigil was held at Footscray Park on 23 March 2017 in response.
perpetrator_info: Groups of men targeting gay beat

title: Kevin Rudd's godson punched for standing up for marriage equality in Brisbane
description: During Australia's marriage equality postal survey, 19-year-old Sean, godson of former PM Kevin Rudd, was punched at a bus stop in Bulimba after confronting a 48-year-old man who was pulling down rainbow flags from a roundabout and yelling homophobic slurs. Sean suffered a significant gash on his head. The attacker was charged with assault and public nuisance. Rudd called it evidence of what the postal vote could unleash.

title: Borna Kazerani assaulted in homophobic attack at Parklea Markets Sydney
description: Borna Kazerani was attacked by Cortez Solomon and Paris Te Atahu Makene Stone in a carpark after visiting Parklea Markets. Solomon punched, kicked, and used homophobic slurs including 'homo' and 'poofter.' Stone pulled Kazerani's hair. Despite calling 000, police never came due to staffing. Solomon was sentenced to 18 months CCO and Stone to 15 months CCO. Magistrate called it 'pretty cowardly.'
perpetrator_info: Cortez Solomon and Paris Te Atahu Makene Stone, sentenced

title: Reality TV star David Subritzky gay bashed on Oxford Street Sydney
location: Oxford Street, Darlinghurst, Sydney, New South Wales
description: Former I'm A Celebrity contestant David Subritzky, 28, was punched in the face by an unidentified man who hurled homophobic slurs at Five Star Kebabs on Oxford Street around 3am. The attacker fled before police arrived. The incident was part of a wave of anti-LGBTQ attacks in Sydney's gaybourhood, prompting NSW Police to deploy more personnel on Oxford Street.

title: Plumber fined for homophobic attack on gay man over rainbow house on Phillip Island
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Plumber Jai Ryan, 24, pleaded guilty to unlawful assault after a homophobic attack on Melbourne hairdresser Mykey O'Halloran at his Phillip Island home. Ryan had verbally attacked O'Halloran over plans to paint his house in rainbow colours. Ryan told police he didn't like gay people because they 'freak him out.' Magistrate called his actions 'uneducated, ignorant and breathlessly stupid.' Ryan was fined $2,500. O'Halloran was later also subject to a machete attack in November.

title: Queensland gay couple sent abusive homophobic letters after marriage equality postal survey result
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] A gay couple in Maroochydore who said they 'fly very much below the radar' received four homophobic letters after the majority Yes result in the marriage equality postal survey. The letters contained horrific homophobic rhetoric and abuse, demonstrating that hate intensified even after the positive vote.

title: David Saint bashed to death in Adelaide south parklands in suspected gay hate crime
description: David Saint, 41, was bashed to death in Adelaide's south parklands on the night of April 16, 1991. Three men were seen assaulting and chasing him across South Terrace before continuing their attack. Two people who tried to intervene were told to mind their own business. Police initially said robbery was the motive, but Saint still had his wallet with $300 cash. The area was a known gay beat and the gay community believed the attack was motivated by homophobia. The murder remains unsolved. A reward of up to $200,000 is on offer via Operation Persist.

title: Shaine Moore strangled to death by ex-boyfriend in Adelaide
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Shaine Moore was found dead in his bedroom, strangled with a shoelace. His ex-boyfriend David Richard Fraser confessed in 2007 to killing him, taking a plea deal for manslaughter claiming an auto-erotic sex game gone wrong. Fraser was released on parole after a few years. In 2009, Fraser killed another boyfriend, Luke Noonan, 29, in similar circumstances and was convicted of murder with a minimum 22 years.
perpetrator_info: David Richard Fraser, convicted of manslaughter

title: Wayne Ruks bashed to death in churchyard in Maryborough Queensland
description: Wayne Ruks, 45, a disability pensioner, was bashed and left for dead in the grounds of St Mary's Catholic Church in Maryborough on the night of July 3, 2008. His body was found by worshippers the next morning. Attackers Richard Meerdink and Jason Pearce claimed Ruks made a homosexual advance, but security camera footage showed no evidence of this. Murder charges were reduced to manslaughter using the controversial "gay panic" defence. Pearce was sentenced to 9 years but released after 4. The case prompted Father Paul Kelly's petition with 290,000 signatures, leading Queensland to abolish the gay panic defence in 2017.
perpetrator_info: Richard Meerdink and Jason Pearce, convicted of manslaughter

title: Gay couple Craig Gillard and Jamie Ronnfeldt bashed by teenagers in Maryborough Queensland
description: Melbourne couple Craig Gillard and Jamie Ronnfeldt were attacked in the early hours of January 17 in Maryborough. One of the youths asked if they had come from a local gay night and called them "faggots" before knocking food out of Gillard's hands. He was tripped and kicked several times. Passer-by Ryan Wolstencroft was attacked when he intervened. Ronnfeldt was knocked down when he tried to call police. Isaac Sanderson and Jason Bishop pled guilty; Dylan Hodge was found guilty of assault causing bodily harm and grievous bodily harm.

title: Anthony Cawsey fatally stabbed in suspected gay hate crime in Centennial Park Sydney
location: Centennial Park, Sydney, New South Wales
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Anthony Cawsey, 37, a stage-hand described as vibrant and charismatic, was found fatally stabbed in Centennial Park in September 2009. He had walked from his Redfern flat to the park, a known gay beat, while phoning a chat line. He was found with a single stab wound to his chest with his pants pulled down. The NSW Special Commission inquiry heard that sexuality was a possible factor. A person of interest, Moses Kellie, was charged in 2015 but the case was dismissed. DNA from three unknown men was found but never matched. The murder remains unsolved.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes

title: Luke Noonan strangled by repeat killer David Fraser in Adelaide
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Luke Noonan, 29, was strangled during a sex act involving erotic asphyxia by David Richard Fraser, 36, in September 2009. Fraser had previously killed his boyfriend Shaine Moore in similar circumstances in 2005 and received only a manslaughter conviction. For Noonan's killing, Fraser initially denied murder but changed his plea to guilty. He was sentenced to murder with a minimum 22 years imprisonment.
perpetrator_info: David Richard Fraser, convicted of murder, minimum 22 years

title: Gay man Joshua and brother bashed by teenagers at Coomera Westfield Gold Coast
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] A gay man named Joshua said he would not return to Queensland after he and his 16-year-old brother were brutally bashed at Coomera Westfield shopping centre on the Gold Coast. The teenagers had earlier yelled homophobic slurs and attacked them on a bus, then hunted them down at the shopping centre to continue the assault.

title: Mark Stewart (Spanswick) - North Head death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 18-year-old Mark Stewart was found at the base of cliffs at North Head, Manly. His death was ruled as multiple injuries from a fall, but the motive remains unclear and is suspected to be a gay-hate crime.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Ernest Head - Summer Hill murder
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 44-year-old Ernest Head was murdered in his apartment, stabbed 35 times. NSW Special Commission arranged re-analysis of exhibits, identifying a known deceased male thought to be involved.
perpetrator_info: Known deceased male identified through palmprint analysis
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155

title: Barry Jones - Five Dock Park murder
location: Five Dock Park, Five Dock, NSW
description: 41-year-old Barry Jones was found with 53 stab wounds and a 10cm throat laceration at Five Dock Park, a known gay beat location.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes

title: Paul Rath - North Head death
description: 27-year-old Paul Rath's body was found near the cliff base at North Head. Initially ruled accidental, but circumstances are suspicious and consistent with gay-hate violence.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: David Lloyd-Williams - North Head death
description: David Lloyd-Williams suffered severe depression and was found deceased at North Head. Case remains unsolved with suspected gay-hate motivation.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Walter John Bedser - Parramatta death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Walter John Bedser, an antique dealer commonly called 'Johnny', was found deceased in Parramatta. Case remains unsolved.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Richard Slater - Birdwood Park assault
description: 69-year-old Richard Slater suffered severe head injuries from an attack at Birdwood Park, a known gay beat location in Newcastle. He died from his injuries at Newcastle Hospital.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Gerald Cuthbert - Paddington murder
description: 27-year-old Gerald Cuthbert was stabbed over 60 times with his throat slit in his Paddington apartment. The extreme frenzied violence is characteristic of gay-hate crimes.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Peter Sheil - Gordons Bay death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 29-year-old Peter Sheil's death circumstances at Gordons Bay/Clovelly cliffs remain unclear. He may have been pushed in a gay-hate attack.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Peter Baumann - Waverley disappearance
description: 25-year-old Peter Baumann, a German national, disappeared from Waverley/Double Bay area. His remains have never been found. The word 'AIDS' was written on a mirror, suggesting anti-gay motivation.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes

title: Wendy Waine - Kings Cross murder
description: Wendy Waine, a transgender woman and entertainer, was shot dead at close range in her Kings Cross apartment. This is a confirmed hate crime targeting her transgender identity.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Gilles Mattaini - Marks Park disappearance
description: 27-year-old Gilles Mattaini, a French national and barman at Menzies Hotel, went for a walk along the Tamarama coastal path and was never seen again. Coroner found he likely met similar fate to Ross Warren and John Russell at the same gay beat. $100,000 reward offered.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; NSW Police $100k Reward; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: William Rooney - Wollongong death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 35-year-old William Rooney, originally from Scotland, suffered fatal facial and head injuries in Wollongong. Suspected gay-hate crime.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: William Allen - Alexandria Park murder
description: 48-year-old William Allen, a retired schoolteacher, was brutally bashed by a gang at Alexandria Park, a known gay beat location.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Russell Payne - Inverell death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 33-year-old Russell Payne died at his home in Inverell. The case remains unsolved with suspected gay-hate motivation.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Samantha Raye - South Head death
description: Samantha Raye, a transgender woman, was found in a coastal cave near Hornby Lighthouse at South Head. Suspected hate crime targeting her gender identity.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: John Hughes - Potts Point murder
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 45-year-old John Hughes was found bound with electrical cord, with a pillow sack over his head and multiple head injuries in his Potts Point apartment.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Ross Warren - Marks Park disappearance
description: 25-year-old Ross Warren, a television news reader for WIN Television, disappeared during a night out at Marks Park. His body was never recovered. Coroner found homicide 'probable'. Part of the 'Gay Gang Murders' at this notorious gay beat. $100,000 reward offered.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; NSW Police $100k Reward; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Graham Paynter - Tathra death
description: 36-year-old Graham Paynter died in Tathra. The case remains unsolved with suspected gay-hate motivation.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: John Russell - Marks Park cliff death
description: 31-year-old John Russell, an openly gay barman at Bronte Bowling Club, was last seen at his farewell drinks at Bondi Hotel. His battered body was found at the bottom of cliffs at Marks Park the next morning. 2005 inquest determined he was thrown from the cliff. Critical evidence (human hair in his hand) was lost by police. $100,000 reward offered.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; NSW Police $100k Reward

title: Richard Johnson - Alexandria Park murder
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] Richard Johnson was beaten and kicked to death in a park toilet block at Alexandria Park. A gang of youths lured him to the toilet where he was bashed and stomped. Eight teens aged 16-18 were charged; three convicted of murder, five of manslaughter (known as 'Alexandria Eight').
perpetrator_info: Eight teenagers aged 16-18 (Alexandria Eight) - three convicted of murder, five of manslaughter
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Simon Blair Wark - Watson's Bay death
description: Simon Blair Wark, an openly gay man, was last seen at Double Bay. His body was found floating off Manly cliffs days later.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Kritchikorn Rattanajaturathaporn - Marks Park murder
description: 31-year-old Kritchikorn Rattanajaturathaporn, a Thai immigrant who arrived in Australia 4 months prior, was attacked with claw hammer, pipe and fists along with companion Geoffrey Sullivan at Marks Park. Kritchikorn was chased along the cliff edge with serious injuries, either pushed or lost footing, and fell to his death. Three men were convicted of assault and murder.
perpetrator_info: Three men convicted of assault and murder
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: William Dutfield - Mosman death
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] 41-year-old William Dutfield was found deceased in his Mosman apartment. The case remains unsolved with suspected gay-hate motivation.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Robert Malcolm - Sydney GPO worker murder
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] 41-year-old Robert Malcolm, known as 'Bob', was a GPO worker who suffered complex skull fractures and severe head injuries in an attack near Eveleigh Street.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Brian Walker - Merrylands death
description: 30-year-old Brian Walker, described as a talented artist, was found deceased in Merrylands. Suspected gay-hate crime.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Crispin Dye - Oxford Street murder
description: 41-year-old Crispin Dye, manager for AC/DC, was found unconscious in a laneway behind Kinselas nightclub after a night out. He died in hospital on Christmas Day from multiple skull fractures. Critical evidence (blood-stained jeans and shirt) was never examined. NSW Special Commission found new DNA evidence identifying an unknown person of interest who died by suicide in 2002.
perpetrator_info: Person of interest identified via DNA (deceased, suicide 2002)
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; AIC TANDI 155; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Kenneth Brennan - Elizabeth Bay murder
description: 53-year-old Kenneth Brennan, an openly gay history teacher, was last seen at a sauna before being stabbed multiple times. Fresh inquest recommended by Special Commission.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Carl Stockton - Bar Cleveland murder
description: 52-year-old Carl Stockton, a train driver, suffered massive head injuries with three separate impact areas at Bar Cleveland in Redfern. Suspected gay-hate crime.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Scott Miller - Millers Point death
description: 21-year-old Scott Miller, who grew up in Orange, was found at the base of a steep drop at Millers Point near Darling Harbour. Suspected gay-hate crime.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Samantha Rose - Kensington murder
description: Samantha Rose, a transgender woman and Westpac computer analyst, was found with skull fractures from blunt force trauma in her Kensington home. Suspected hate crime targeting her transgender identity.
cross_reference: NSW Special Commission of Inquiry into LGBTIQ Hate Crimes; Strike Force Parrabell (NSW Police, 2018)

title: Andrew Barnard - C3 Church conversion practices survivor
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] Andrew Barnard, a former member of C3 Church in Sydney, was subjected to conversion practices from age 13. He twice attempted suicide after church therapists told him his sexuality was due to 'nurture' and childhood experiences. He suffered severe depression, internalized homophobia, and self-loathing.

title: Anthony Venn-Brown - Assemblies of God conversion practices (1972-1999)
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] Anthony Venn-Brown OAM was subjected to conversion practices for 27 years after attending a church meeting in Adelaide featuring 'ex-gay' speaker Sy Rogers. In 2022, a church officially apologized for the harm caused. Venn-Brown became a leading advocate for banning conversion practices in Australia.

title: Trans woman subjected to conversion practices - Melbourne
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] A transgender woman was subjected to conversion practices in Melbourne by a religious organization that attempted to change her gender identity. The practices caused lasting psychological trauma and mental health issues.

title: Victoria conversion practices survivor testimony - Religious setting
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] Survivor testimony documented by Victorian Equal Opportunity and Human Rights Commission of change or suppression practices in religious settings. Victim experienced pressure to change their sexual orientation through prayer, counselling and pastoral interventions.
cross_reference: VEOHRC Change or Suppression Practices documentation

title: NSW conversion practices - 40 survivors lodge submissions
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] Around 40 survivors of conversion practices in NSW lodged submissions and attended roundtables during the campaign to ban conversion therapy. Their testimonies documented practices in religious, pastoral, and informal settings causing long-term psychological harm, trauma, shame, and isolation.
perpetrator_info: Various religious and pastoral organizations in NSW
cross_reference: Equality Australia; NSW Conversion Practices Ban Bill 2024

title: Hillsong Church conversion practices allegations - Sydney

title: Gerard Fleming - Narrabeen gay beat murder
description: 35-year-old Gerard Fleming, a gay man with autism, was fatally stabbed at a public toilet block (known gay beat) in Narrabeen by a 16-year-old who used the homosexual advance defence. This case became a landmark example in the campaign to abolish the gay panic defence in NSW, achieved in 2014.

title: Gay bisexual Wiradjuri teenager lured via Wizz app and bashed at Strathfield Park by IS-inspired teen gang
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - source states 'March 2024', no specific day confirmed.] [NOTE: Location confirmed as Strathfield Park per ABC court records, but exact spot within park unknown.] 16-year-old bisexual Wiradjuri teenager ('James') was lured via Wizz dating app by a 17-year-old posing as 'Johnny' and picked up from Liverpool Station. Four perpetrators aged 14-17 ambushed and bashed him at Strathfield Park. One attacker wore a hoodie bearing the Shahada symbol. Victim was kicked in the back, legs, chest and face and was hospitalised. Attack was filmed on camera and circulated. Five teenagers convicted; 'Johnny' sentenced to a minimum six months in youth detention for four attacks. Perpetrators linked to IS-inspired extremism and Al Madina Dawah Centre, Bankstown. [NOTE: This incident is likely captured in NSW Police official hate crime data recording 23 LGBT offences in Strathfield (January 2023 – October 2025). Source: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-27/nsw-lgbt-hate-data-released-ahead-of-mardi-gras/106394004]
perpetrator_info: Four teenagers aged 14-17; five convicted in total; 'Johnny' (17) sentenced to minimum six months youth detention; linked to Al Madina Dawah Centre (Bankstown) and IS-inspired extremism
cross_reference: Al Madina Dawah Centre; ABC Investigation 2026-02-25

title: 64+ charged in NSW and Victoria for IS and far-right inspired app-based attacks on LGBTQIA+ people since 2023
location: Sydney, NSW (exact location unknown - systemic pattern, not a single incident)
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only the year was confirmed in the source; mapped to 1 January for display purposes.] [NOTE: Location approximate - exact address unknown; mapped to the nearest known area for display purposes.] [NOTE: This entry documents a systemic pattern, not a single incident. Date 2023-01-01 is the approximate start of the documented pattern, not a specific event date. Mapped to Sydney as a reference point only - attacks occurred across NSW, VIC, ACT, QLD, and WA.] ABC two-year investigation found at least 64 people charged in NSW and Victoria since 2023 for app-based attacks on LGBTQIA+ people. Victims lured via Grindr and Wizz dating apps and bashed on camera. Perpetrators radicalised by a mix of IS jihadism and far-right ideology. Several congregated around Al Madina Dawah Centre in Bankstown (ordered to close after the Bondi Beach Hanukkah massacre). Spiritual leader Wisam Haddad delivered lectures describing homosexuality as 'punishable by death'; recruiter Wassim Fayad was released from a control order in 2023 despite identification as an IS cell member. Similar attacks also reported in ACT, Queensland, and Western Australia. Police acknowledge many incidents go unreported.
perpetrator_info: 64+ charged across NSW and Victoria; teenagers predominantly; linked to Al Madina Dawah Centre (Bankstown); influenced by Wisam Haddad and Wassim Fayad; IS terrorist network
cross_reference: Al Madina Dawah Centre; ABC Investigation 2026-02-25; Bondi Beach Hanukkah massacre

title: Jack Jacobs lured via Grindr and bashed near Fitzroy Gardens Melbourne
description: [NOTE: Date approximate - only month and year were confirmed in the source; mapped to the 1st of the month for display purposes.] Jack Jacobs was lured to a meeting near Fitzroy Gardens via a Grindr profile that subsequently disappeared. He was ambushed and violently attacked by a group of approximately 8-9 teenage boys. He suffered head injuries causing temporary vision loss during the attack, wrist injuries, and two torn ligaments requiring surgery. Jacobs did not initially report it as a hate crime. When he later attempted formal reporting through Victoria Police's LGBTIQA+ Liaison Officers, his original statement had not been documented, making the process lengthy and difficult to pursue. No charges were filed.
perpetrator_info: Group of approximately 8-9 teenage boys; no charges filed
